testName: Load dashboard, navigate to skill, play video

fix.summary: After opening the Outline drawer, MUI Tooltip Fade animation can produce a false color-contrast violation. Wait for the tooltip's opacity to reach 1 before re-running axe, and exclude the tooltip popper from the second scan.
fix.pattern: Before the second cy.checkA11yViolations(...): if ($body => $body.find('.MuiTooltip-tooltip').length) { cy.get('.MuiTooltip-tooltip').should('have.css', 'opacity', '1'); }; then cy.checkA11yViolations({ exclude: ['.MuiTooltip-popper'] }). Don't change product CSS to chase contrast — verify the scan is sampling steady-state first.

investigationFindings: Commits c09463fa4 / 30eb06c40 explicitly exclude `.MuiTooltip-popper` and wait for opacity so axe doesn't sample the mid-Fade frame. This is a recurring false positive tied to MUI tooltip transitions, not to actual app contrast.
rootCauseChain: ASSERTION_MISMATCH -> axe samples a transient mid-Fade tooltip frame -> spurious color-contrast violation
